why: Tier3 TV feasible=False; n_eff_q25_tv=112 vs required≈252 (tv_window≈84)
Tier3 STATIC feasible=True; n_eff_q25_static=109 vs required≈90
Non-stationarity is not a blocker if TIME_VARYING_gVAR is feasible; it mainly affects STATIC feasibility.
overall.technical_summary: Tier=Tier3_LaggedDynamicNetwork (STATIC_gVAR); score=94.6/100 (FullyReady). Vars: candidates=14, ready=6, hard_drop=0, soft_drop=8-9. Time: regularity=regular_or_quasi_regular, irregular_frac=0.00, dup_frac=0.00, sorted=True. Tier3 STATIC: n_eff_q25=109, required≈90; feasible=True. Tier3 TV: n_eff_q25=112, required≈252; tv_window≈84; feasible=False. Non-stationarity detected in several variables; STATIC_gVAR is feasible but with caveats. Time-varying gVAR is not feasible with current data (252 required points). Mean missingness ≈21.1%. Ready vars=6 out of 14 candidates; overall readiness is high though nonstationarity and missingness warrant careful preprocessing and documentation.
overall.client_friendly_summary: Your data is nearly ready for a time-lagged network model, with a static (non-changing over time) variant that works now. To keep things reliable and potentially unlock a time-varying version later, we should gather more data and reduce gaps. Right now, 6 variables are usable out of 14, with regular timing and about 21% missing data. A longer, denser dataset would let us explore a dynamic model that changes over time.
next_steps: Increase data completeness to reduce missingness.
Prioritize data collection for the six ready variables (P01, P08, C02, C03, C04, C06).
Investigate preprocessing to address non-stationarity (e.g., detrending or differencing).
Consider smoothing/windowing strategies if pursuing TIME_VARYING_gVAR with more data.
Document decisions and rationale in a reproducible report.
Plan a longer time-series collection to reach ~252 points required for TIME_VARYING_gVAR feasibility.
caveats: Non-stationarity detected in several variables; STATIC_gVAR assumes stationarity and may bias estimates if not addressed.
Six ready variables limit model scope and may omit important dynamics.
Missingness ~21% can reduce power and affect stability of estimates; imputation or thresholding may be required.
Time-varying gVAR is currently not feasible with the available data (252 points needed).
Results depend on chosen correlation method (Spearman) and assumptions about data regularity.
Some nonstationary signals (e.g., P02, P03, P04, P05) warrant preprocessing before static modeling.
Low perceived high-collinearity risk reported; continue monitoring as model variables change.
Documentation should explicitly state stationarity considerations and preprocessing steps to maintain reproducibility.

nonstationarity_handling.statement: Non-stationarity is not a problem if TIME_VARYING_gVAR is feasible; it mainly limits STATIC_gVAR feasibility.
